title: Vanishing Gradient Playground
tagline: Watch gradients decay — or survive — through deep networks
summary: Slide network depth from 1 to 50 layers. Toggle the activation between sigmoid, tanh, and ReLU. Watch gradient magnitudes at each layer on a log scale. Sigmoid collapses past 20 layers; ReLU stays alive. One slider, one visceral demo of why we abandoned sigmoid for deep networks.
highlights: One slider for depth, three activation buttons — every combination is live.
Color-coded bars: green when gradients are healthy, yellow when they are shrinking, red when they are effectively zero.
Dramatic collapse for sigmoid past 20 layers: 0.25^20 ≈ 10^-12, literally below float precision.
See why ReLU changed deep learning: gradients stay at order 10^-1 even at 50 layers.
Live readout of the input-layer gradient in scientific notation.
